name: Linear & Affine Types
def: Types tracking how many times a value may be used — exactly once (linear) or at most once (affine).
problem: Statically guarantee resources (memory, file handles, sessions) are used and released correctly.
mechanism: The type system tracks ownership/usage; a moved value cannot be reused. Rust’s ownership is an affine system enforcing single ownership and move semantics.
benefits: Prevents use-after-free / double-free at compile time
No GC needed for memory safety
limits: Some patterns (graphs, shared mutability) are awkward
failures: Fighting the borrow checker into Rc/RefCell escape hatches
Encoding leaks in languages without support
whenNot: Quick scripts; GC languages where it adds friction
myths: "Linear and affine are the same" — linear requires use; affine merely permits at most one use
